mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals tightening labor market conditions that boost consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and new housing starts indicates broad-based economic expansion that lifts corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% per quarter over three consecutive rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and supports corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 for three consecutive quarters, indicating the supply signal has lost predictive power for short-term equity moves.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI exceeding 0.5 percent triggers inflation hedging demands and anticipates aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal yields positive 20-day forward returns in over 60% of triggered episodes across a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in residential construction spending directly measures household investment activity and construction sector health, which correlates with rising corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year Spearman correlation between RSAFS changes and 60-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously increasing unemployment rate indicates labor force participation expansion rather than genuine job creation, which signals weak underlying demand and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to generate a statistically significant negative alpha (p > 0.10) relative to a market-neutral benchmark over two full market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 1000 thousand units signals a contraction in the housing sector and broader consumer confidence, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The threshold signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of historical instances where HOUST drops below the 1000 threshold.
